name: Tajik Non
recipeIngredient: 1 cup plain yogurt (2 percent or whole milk)
2 1/2 cups unbleached all-purpose or hard white flour (2 1/2 to 3 1/2)
recipeInstructions:
Topping:
Approximately 2 tablespoons finely chopped shallot
Approximately 1 teaspoon salt
Water for spritzing
You will need a large bread bowl, small heavy-bottomed saucepan, unglazed quarry tiles or baking stone or one or two baking sheets to fit on a rack in your oven, rolling pin, bread stamp or fork or wire whisk, peel or long-handled spatula, and small spray bottle for spritzing (optional).
1. In a large bowl, combine 1/2 cup warm water with sugar. Stir in yeast and let it dissolve.
2. In a saucepan, mix yogurt and 1 cup water until combined. Bring to medium heat and stir occasionally until lukewarm.
3. Add the yogurt mixture to the yeast mixture.
4. Gradually add whole wheat flour one cup at a time, stirring only in one direction as you incorporate each addition.
5. Stir the dough for about 100 revolutions (approximately one minute) in the same direction to activate gluten development.
6. Cover and let the sponge rest, covered, for 10 minutes or up to 2 hours.
7. Sprinkle salt over the sponge, then gradually add unbleached flour until the dough is stiff enough not to stir easily.
8. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 10 minutes, making it smooth and elastic while adding more flour as needed to prevent sticking.
9. Rinse, wipe, and lightly oil your bread bowl.
10. Place dough in the bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise until doubled in size, about 1 1/2 hours.
11. Preheat oven rack to the top third of the oven. Arrange unglazed quarry tiles on it, leaving a 1-inch gap between the tiles and oven walls for air circulation.
12. Alternatively, place baking stone or sheets on the rack. Heat oven to 500°F.
13. Gently punch down the dough and turn out onto a lightly floured surface. If time allows, let rest, covered, for 10 minutes.
14. Divide dough into 8 equal pieces using lightly floured hands.
15. Press each piece into a round measuring about 3-4 inches in diameter.
16. Work with one round at a time, keeping the others covered on your work surface.
17. Roll out to a 6-inch round. Using a fork or bread stamp, or the edge of a wire whisk, make an imprint of a circle about 1 1/2 inches in diameter in the center.
18. Flatten and puncture the dough by pressing down thoroughly.
19. Sprinkle chopped shallot, salt, and water onto the center.
20. Quickly roll out and prepare additional breads as needed, then transfer to hot tiles, stone, or baking sheets.
21. Bake for about 5 minutes, or until lightly golden; for a deeper color, bake up to 7-8 minutes.
22. Remove with a spatula from oven and place on rack to cool. Stack and loosely wrap in cloth to keep soft and warm.
23. Shape and bake remaining breads the same way.
Yield: 8 breads approximately 6 inches in diameter with a soft, raised rim and flattened center topped with shallots
